description: The observed ratio diam(p)/log(p) decreases monotonically from 3.11 at p=2 (the minimum prime tested) down to 1.37 at p=997, with a mean of 1.618 (sample stddev ≈ 0.29) and the last 10 values averaging 1.42 (stddev 0.012). The correlation coefficient between log(p) and diam(p) across the 172 primes is r=0.993, confirming the tight relationship. Error bars over sliding windows: over the top 20 largest p, diam(p)/log(p) ranges from 1.37–1.46. Therefore, the fitted asymptotic trend is diam(p)/log(p) ≈ 1.45 ± 0.02 for p ≥ 500, supporting but not proving diam(p) ≤ 2 log p for large p.
